**Author:** AJ Cassata
**Video title:** Why Most B2B Outreach FAILS And What Actually Works to Scale Sales
**Channel/show:** GO4CEO Podcast
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NXrnVi1mG4Y
**Upload date:** 2026-01-28
**Transcript source:** YouTube auto-generated English captions (via yt-dlp) — summarized, not reproduced verbatim, due to copyright
**Playbook relevance:** Common failure modes chapter

---

## Summary

This episode is mostly a founder-journey interview rather than a tactical outbound how-to — useful for understanding *why* AJ's frameworks exist, less so for copy/list tactics.

- **Origin story:** Cassata started Revenue Boost after growing a previous local-marketing agency purely through cold email/LinkedIn outreach, then noticed peers kept asking him how he did it — which became the paid consulting/agency business.
- **Niche discovery:** Moved from local service businesses toward B2B SaaS/tech and agencies specifically because those companies often can't justify paid ads (thin margins, small target markets) and lean on outbound instead.
- **Team/hiring philosophy:** Stayed lean, hired a personal assistant first, then mentored (rather than hired externally for) a head of fulfillment — deliberately avoiding over-hiring during short-term revenue spikes, a mistake he made in his first business.
- **Financial discipline:** Keeps 6–12 months of runway and won't make an investment unless he's confident it pays for itself within a year; learned this after having to lay off staff following a couple of bad months.
- **AI workflow:** Builds custom GPTs/Claude Projects trained on his own top-performing emails and internal knowledge rather than using generic ChatGPT prompts, because generic prompts "scrape the web for the most popular cold email templates," producing generic output. A human always reviews AI output before it's sent — nothing is 100% automated.
- **Testing cadence:** Says a new client's campaigns typically take 3–6 months of testing (different lists, sequences, offers) before finding what reliably works — pushes back on the idea that outbound is "one and done."
- **Personal productivity:** Frames founder productivity like an athlete's — health, mindset, and routine directly determine output quality, and he deliberately does not work 7 days a week.

## Notable quotes

> "You get more of a sense for what's going to land, but you ultimately don't know until you actually just test and put something in front of the audience."

> "If there's anything that I do repeatedly in my business, I'm always making a bot for it so that it just gets really good."
